_comment_1: Two variants below — pick ONE based on how OpenClaw runs. Merge under mcp.servers in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json, OR use: openclaw mcp set lark-cli '<entry as JSON>'
_comment_2: VARIANT A — OpenClaw runs ON THE HOST (same machine as lark-cli). Use stdio for least moving parts.
_comment_3: VARIANT B — OpenClaw runs IN DOCKER and lark-cli is on the host. Use HTTP. Run on host: `lark-cli mcp serve --transport http --addr 127.0.0.1:3000`. Container reaches host via host.docker.internal.

lark-cli-stdio._use_when: VARIANT A — OpenClaw runs on the same machine as the lark-cli binary.

lark-cli-http._use_when: VARIANT B — OpenClaw runs in Docker; lark-cli is installed on the macOS/Linux host outside the container.
